title: palaquium gutta
intro: Palaquium gutta, also known as the Gutta Percha tree, is a tropical evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Islands, characterized by its smooth, grey bark and oval-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to reduce swelling and fight off infections. The health benefits of Palaquium gutta include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in pharmaceutical applications.
